releases.name: v1.2.0

releases.author: THANSHEER
releases.description:
**Mermaid Flow** is a visual, WYSIWYG editor for Mermaid flowcharts — build and rearrange diagrams by dragging nodes and drawing connections, with no Mermaid syntax required.

## ✨ Features
- 🖱️ **Drag-and-drop canvas** — move nodes and draw connections with your mouse
- 🔣 **Syntax-free** — the plugin writes the Mermaid code for you
- 🎨 **Full styling** — fill, stroke & text colors, fonts, and 14 node shapes
- 🧩 **Subgraphs, edge labels, and link styles**
- 📌 **Persistent layouts** — manual node positions are saved in hidden Mermaid comments, so edits never scramble your diagram
- 🪟 **Popup or embedded pane** — edit in a focused dialog or in a tab beside your note
- 👁️ **Reading Mode & Live Preview** — an **Edit** button appears on any rendered diagram
- 📤 **Export** to SVG / PNG and copy to clipboard

## 🚀 Getting started
1. Insert a diagram via the ribbon icon or the command **Mermaid Flow: Insert visual Mermaid diagram**.
2. Edit any existing ` ```mermaid ` block with its **Edit** button, or place your cursor inside one and run **Mermaid Flow: Edit Mermaid diagram visually**.

## 📦 Installation
**Community plugins:** Settings → Community plugins → Browse → search **"Mermaid Flow"** → Install → Enable.

**Manual:** download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` from the assets below into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/mermaid-flow/`, then enable it in Settings.

## Requirements
Obsidian **v1.7.2** or newer.

---
🐛 Issues & feedback welcome → https://github.com/THANSHEER
releases.name: Mermaid Flow 1.2.0 — Visual flowchart editor for Obsidian

releases.author: THANSHEER
releases.description:
Long Description (for the plugin page):
  > Mermaid Flow is the ultimate visual companion for Mermaid diagrams
  in Obsidian. It bridges the gap between text-based charting and
  visual brainstorming.
  >
  > Why use Mermaid Flow?
  > - Speed: Build flowcharts in seconds by dragging and dropping
  instead of typing.
  > - Precision: Unlike standard Mermaid, Mermaid Flow remembers
  exactly where you placed your nodes.
  > - Flexibility: Work the way you want with support for both Popup
  and Split-Pane editing.
  > - Native Feel: Designed to look and feel like a core Obsidian
  feature, supporting both Light and Dark themes.
  >
  > How to get started:
  > 1. Use the ribbon icon or the command Insert visual Mermaid
  diagram.
  > 2. To edit an existing diagram, simply click the "Edit" button
  that appears on any Mermaid block.
releases.name: Create and edit Mermaid flowcharts visually. Drag-and-drop nodes,   draw connections, and save layouts without writing syntax.
